title: Reflux surgical clinic documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports adult recurrent epigastric pain, heartburn, acid regurgitation, bloating, nausea or vomiting, associated precipitants, meal timing, and clinician-discussed lifestyle documentation.
assessment.rationale: Supports heartburn, regurgitation, dyspepsia, nausea, meal and precipitant context for reflux surgical clinic documentation; no decision is generated.

source.title: Gastro-oesophageal reflux disease and dyspepsia in adults: investigation and management
source.population: Adults aged 18 years and over with dyspepsia, symptoms suggestive of gastro-oesophageal reflux disease, or both.
source.setting: Community, primary-care, investigation, review, and specialist referral.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected adult reflux and dyspepsia symptom, alarm, investigation, review, and clinician-discussed management fields; the legacy workflow has no adult-only filter and UAE pathways require clinician review.

exact_location.heading: Scope and recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.2 and 1.4.1 — symptom profile and common care

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports adult recurrent epigastric pain, heartburn, acid regurgitation, bloating, nausea or vomiting, associated precipitants, meal timing, and clinician-discussed lifestyle documentation.

assessment.text: Supports immediate specialist referral for significant acute gastrointestinal bleeding, medicine-cause review, prior endoscopy review, and further assessment when new alarm signs are present.
assessment.rationale: Supports bleeding, medicine-cause, prior endoscopy and alarm-feature review for reflux surgical clinic documentation; no decision is generated.

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports immediate specialist referral for significant acute gastrointestinal bleeding, medicine-cause review, prior endoscopy review, and further assessment when new alarm signs are present.

assessment.text: Supports clinician-documented reflux management, medication-plan discussion, review, and specialist referral where symptoms do not respond or remain unexplained; no dose or autonomous treatment text is mapped.
assessment.rationale: Supports clinician-documented management and specialist-referral context for reflux surgical clinic documentation; no decision is generated.
